content:
# Session 253 (iter-253) ‚Äî review summary

## Metadata
- **Iteration / session:** iter-253 / session_253. Model: `opus` (both lanes), mode `prove`.
- **Lanes:** M=2 ‚Äî Lane TS-cmp (`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, D1‚Ä≤) + Lane TS-inv (`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean`, dual-inverse chain).
- **Sorry counts (term-level, this iter):**
  - `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`: **3 ‚Üí 3** (L708 `exists_tensorObj_inverse` guardrailed; L1962 `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural` STEP A; L2027 `pullbackTensorMap_natural` STEP B/D1‚Ä≤).
  - `DualInverse.lean`: **2 ‚Üí 3** by occurrence (L256 `dual_restrict_iso` Step-4; L565 `homOfLocalCompat` (a); L581 `homOfLocalCompat` (c)). The prover framed this as "2 ‚Üí 2" by *declarations-carrying-sorry* (homOfLocalCompat=1 decl, dual_restrict_iso=1 decl): it closed sub-step (b) of the single iter-252 scaffold sorry but split the remainder into named sub-sorries (a) and (c). Honest occurrence count is 2‚Üí3; honest decl-count is 2‚Üí2. Not laundering ‚Äî real code was added (see below).
- **Targets closed this iter: NONE.** This is the **3rd consecutive M=2 iter (251, 252, 253) with zero target closures.**
- **Build:** `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` compiles clean (verified first-hand: `lean_verify pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` ‚Üí `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`). `DualInverse.lean` could not be built in-place for most of the session (sibling import broken mid-race) ‚Äî its facts were scratch-verified in an isolated `import Mathlib` file.
- **Non-regression confirmed:** the load-bearing prior win **D2‚Ä≤ (`pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso`, closed iter-250) is still axiom-clean.** The L481 "opaque" warning is the known iter-250 prose-comment false-positive.

## Lane TS-cmp ‚Äî `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural` (STEP A) + `pullbackTensorMap_natural` (STEP B/D1‚Ä≤)

### STEP A (L1962) ‚Äî BLOCKED; the armed REVERSING SIGNAL fired
The plan armed a binary signal: *if STEP A does not close even with the in-file element recipe + the bw253b blueprint roadmap, the "mechanical residual" read is wrong.* **It fired.** STEP A did not close.

Three approaches, all failed, root cause identical (the iter-250 `restrictScalars (ùüô)`-over-sheafification `whnf` hazard):
1. **Element-descent** (`apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; ext x; induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on`, the iter-252 pivot route + bw253b roadmap). The `tmul` case reduces to an explicit instance-free `TensorProduct` element identity, but closing it re-introduces the sectionwise Œ∑-naturality which re-introduces the instance split. LSP said clean; the real build did not close.
2. **Whisker calculus** on the post-`congr 1` presheaf identity `(p ‚äó‚Çò q) ‚â´ (Œ∑_{P'}‚ñ∑Q' ‚â´ (aP').val‚óÅŒ∑_{Q'}) = (Œ∑_P‚ñ∑Q ‚â´ (aP).val‚óÅŒ∑_Q) ‚â´ ((a.map p).val ‚äó‚Çò (a.map q).val)`, via `rw [tensorHom_def p q, tensorHom_def]` then `erw [whisker_exchange, ‚Üê comp_whiskerRight_assoc, ‚Üê whiskerLeft_comp, hp, hq, ‚Ä¶]`. Mathematically complete by hand. **Lean error: `(deterministic) timeout at whnf, maximum heartbeats (6400000)`** ‚Äî `erw`'s keyed-defeq does `whnf` on `restrictScalars (ùüô) (aP').val` over sheafification and never terminates. Plain `rw [Category.assoc]`/`whisker_exchange` fail to even match ("pattern not found") because the goal carries two defeq-but-distinct monoidal `‚â´` instances (`monoidalCategoryStruct` from `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq` vs `monoidalCategory.toMonoidalCategoryStruct` from `tensorHom_def`).
3. **Uniform-instance helper extraction** (`sheafifyUnitWhiskerPaste`, restated over `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` so `whisker_exchange` runs by plain `rw`). **Fails at the statement level: 4√ó `synthInstanceFailed` ‚Äî `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` cannot be synthesized** (that ring-functor spelling lacks the monoidal instance; only the defeq-massaged in-context term has it).

**The obstacle is pure Lean tactic friction, not a Mathlib gap** ‚Äî the hand-derivation is complete. The root is the recurring `restrictScalars (ùüô)`-over-sheafification `whnf` wall, now hit via the `erw` `‚â´`-instance bridge.

### STEP B / D1‚Ä≤ (L2027) ‚Äî genuine partial advance, blocked on the Sheaf.val/.obj merge
Advanced from bare `simp only [pullbackTensorMap, tensorObj_functoriality]; sorry` to a compiling **Square 1 (`sheafificationCompPullback` naturality, `erw [‚Ä¶ .hom.naturality_assoc]`) + setup**. The full 4-square chain is authored in-file (S2 `Œ¥_natural`, S3 STEP A, S4 `pullbackValIso_hom_natural` ‚Äî the latter CLOSED). **Blocker:** the Square-2 merge ‚Äî `Functor.comp_map` spells the first `a_Y` as `sheafification (ùüô Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` while the `Œ¥`-factor reads `sheafification (ùüô (Sheaf.val Y.ringCatSheaf))` (SAME functor, `Sheaf.val = .obj`), and `‚Üê Functor.map_comp` will not merge; `rw [show Sheaf.val = .obj from rfl]` ‚Üí "motive is not type correct"; `dsimp only [Sheaf.val]` leaves the two normal forms distinct. D1‚Ä≤ is also fundamentally gated on STEP A (its S3 cites it).

## Lane TS-inv ‚Äî `homOfLocalCompat` (PRIMARY) + `dual_restrict_iso` Step-4

### homOfLocalCompat ‚Äî sub-step (b) CLOSED; sub-step (a) hits a SIGNATURE-RESTRUCTURE blocker
- **Sub-step (b) CLOSED.** Two new compiling helpers: `topSectionToHom` (converts a section of `presheafHom F G` over the terminal `op ‚ä§` into a global `F ‚ü∂ G`, since `‚ä§` is terminal in `Opens X`) + `topSectionToHom_app`. The glue is `refine homMk (topSectionToHom (hsup ‚ñ∏ (hglue hcompat).choose)) ?_` (‚àÉ! is a `Prop` ‚Üí extract via `.choose`; `hsup ‚ñ∏` transports `op (iSup U) ‚Üí op ‚ä§`).
- **Sub-step (a) IsCompatible/cocycle (L565) ‚Äî BLOCKED on `hf`.** The goal was reduced (verified in-context) to the explicit sectionwise core equation `M.map(eqToHom) ‚â´ (f i).val.app ‚â´ N.map(eqToHom) = M.map(eqToHom) ‚â´ (f j).val.app ‚â´ N.map(eqToHom)`. The agreement of the two conjugated section maps must come from `hf`, which is phrased as **an `HEq` between `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images** `gi = (pullback (resLE (ùüô X)(U i)(U i‚äìU j))).map (f i)` and `gj` (analogous j). Consuming this HEq into an `Eq` needs the source/target object equalities, but **each object is a sheafification of a pullback presheaf ‚Äî only isomorphic, not propositionally equal** (scratch-verified): `rfl` fails, `exact?` fails, `congr 1` exposes the false residual `(‚ÜëU i).Modules = (‚ÜëU j).Modules` (different source categories), and no `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules` HEq-extensionality exists. `Subsingleton.elim` only collapses the thin `Opens` route-difference, not the non-subsingleton `Ab` hom-set agreement of `f i` vs `f j`. So `hf` (as phrased) is unconsumable ‚Äî arguably not even satisfiable by a caller.
  - **Important:** `homOfLocalCompat` is **NOT** in `archon-protected.yaml`. The prover called `hf` "PROTECTED" but it is not formally frozen ‚Äî re-phrasing it is a planner / blueprint / refactor decision the autonomous loop can make itself. Recommended re-phrasings (both make (a) short): **(1) sectionwise** (the exact core eqn the goal reduces to ‚Äî bridge becomes `rfl`/direct), or **(2)** via `restrictFunctor` + `restrictFunctorComp`/`eqToHom` transport of `f i`, `f j` into the common object `M.restrict (U i ‚äì U j).Œπ` (`restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` are `rfl`).
- **Sub-step (c) linearity (L581)** ‚Äî transitively gated on (a) (the glued section only exists once `hcompat` is supplied). The argument itself (sectionwise via the `IsGluing` datum + `N.val`-separatedness) is independent and writable once (a)/the signature lands.

### dual_restrict_iso Step-4 (L256) ‚Äî not attempted (can't-verify environment)
The sibling `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` was non-compiling the whole session (TS-cmp race, hard error at L1995/L1911, no parent `.olean`), so `DualInverse.lean` reported `failed_dependencies` and could not be `lake build`'d. The prover deferred rather than inject elaborate unverifiable carrier-heavy code. Only cleanup landed: corrected a stale L322‚Äì323 note (`dual_unit_iso` is CLOSED axiom-clean, not "a small sorry if needed").

## Key findings / patterns

1. **The armed REVERSING SIGNAL on STEP A fired** ‚Äî the "mechanical residual" read was wrong; STEP A is friction-bound across 3 approaches, all rooted in the `restrictScalars (ùüô)`-over-sheafification `whnf` wall. Next move is the pre-armed pc253b SECONDARY (mathlib-analogist consult), **NOT** a 4th targeted retry.
2. **DualInverse Route 2 has a structural signature blocker** (`hf` HEq-of-pullback-images unconsumable). This blocks `homOfLocalCompat` fundamentally and is NOT a proof-filling task. Needs a planner/blueprint/refactor decision to re-sign `hf`.
3. **TOOLING HAZARD (cost most of the TS-cmp session):** `lean_diagnostic_messages` (range-limited) and `lean_multi_attempt` returned **STALE / CACHED** "clean" results on the two heavy lemmas; only `lake env lean <file>` was reliable. Twice STEP A/B looked CLOSED in the LSP and the real build revealed a `whnf` timeout / `rewrite` failure. (Recorded in memory `ts-d1prime-whnf-wall-iter253`.)
4. **Parallel-lane race hazard recurred:** the TS-cmp lane left `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` non-compiling mid-session ‚Üí `DualInverse.lean` (imports it) could not be verified in-place all session. Both files build clean post-session.
5. **`sync_leanok` net ‚àí14 (16 removed / 2 added)** at sha `beec0117`, chapters `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex` + `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`. The iter-252 KB predicted iter-253's sync would *restore* markers stripped by the prior race; instead it stripped more. Most likely the same parallel-race / stale-olean artifact at sync time (not a Lean regression ‚Äî D2‚Ä≤ verified axiom-clean first-hand). **Flagged as an ambiguity**, not CRITICAL (sync_leanok-state.json `iter == 253`, so the markers are the script's current deterministic verdict). The next plan agent should confirm per-decl marker restoration on the genuinely-closed proofs in both chapters.

## Blueprint-doctor
**CLEAN** ‚Äî no orphan chapters, no broken `\ref`/`\uses`, no new `axiom`s. (The recurring `\uses{\leanok}` corruption did not recur.)

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- None this iter. No Mathlib-backed re-export was added (the new `topSectionToHom`/`topSectionToHom_app` are project-local defs, not aliases); no prover rename flagged a `\lean{...}` correction; no `\notready` blocks the prover landed; `\leanok` is the deterministic sync's domain.

## Subagent reports (this iter)
- `lean-auditor` aud253 ‚Äî see `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-aud253.md` (findings folded into recommendations.md).
- `lean-vs-blueprint-checker` di253 ‚Äî see `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-di253.md` (DualInverse vs `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`; the `hf`-bridge adequacy question ‚Äî findings folded into recommendations.md).

## Recommendations for the next plan iter
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: **STOP re-dispatching both lanes against their current shapes.** STEP A ‚Üí mathlib-analogist consult (the armed corrective; consider cross-domain on the carrier shape, given 3 iters of the same root). `homOfLocalCompat` ‚Üí re-sign `hf` (sectionwise or restrictFunctor transport) + update the blueprint, before any re-dispatch. D1‚Ä≤ gated on STEP A. `dual_restrict_iso` needs a stable sibling.
